content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the rlm.5 changes in `src/cli/run.rs` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` and inspected the new tests. The detached-owner fallback and corrupt-lease quarantine updates look sound, but the new Phase 0 orphaned-lease repair path still has an incomplete daemon `run.pid` cleanup contract.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:440-451` now treats missing-worktree-lease repair as successful as soon as `clear_orphaned_task_lease_reference()` returns, but `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3673-3695` only removes `run.pid` when the pid record belongs to the current daemon PID and suppresses all pid-file read/remove errors. In the real partial-cleanup case this branch is meant to repair, a previous daemon process can already have removed the lease record and died before clearing task metadata; when a later daemon runs Phase 0, it will clear `task.lease_id` and `label_dirty` but leave the stale daemon-owned `run.pid` behind because `pid_record.pid != std::process::id()`. The same code also reports success if pid-file deletion itself fails. Please make orphaned cleanup treat unresolved pid cleanup as a partial failure and reclaim dead daemon-owned pid records from prior daemon processes instead of only the current PID.
   Rationale: This still allows the repo to be marked repaired while contradictory durable state remains on disk (`task.lease_id` cleared but stale daemon `run.pid` present), which is the stale-state class this bead is trying to eliminate.
